signals.headline: US, Iran Eye Fresh Talks Before Ceasefire Expires Next Week | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US, Iran Eye Fresh Talks Before Ceasefire Expires Next Week | NO | HIGH confidence**  
No new ceasefire confirmation, announcement, or resolution today; weekend talks in Islamabad ended without a deal, but both sides are discussing another round of negotiations before the existing two-week ceasefire—announced April 7—expires early next week.[1]

signals.headline: AMRO Raises South Korea 2026 Inflation Forecast to 2
signals.raw: AMRO Raises South Korea 2026 Inflation Forecast to 2.3% Due to Global Energy Prices | YES | MED confidence**[1]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: **Details**: On April 6, 2026, the ASEAN+3 Macroeconomic Research Office (AMRO) released its '2026 Regional Economic Out
signals.raw: **Details**: On April 6, 2026, the ASEAN+3 Macroeconomic Research Office (AMRO) released its '2026 Regional Economic Outlook' report, increasing South Korea's annual inflation forecast for 2026 from 1.9% (last month) to 2.3%, citing rises in global energy prices from the U.S.-Iran war. This exceeds the Bank of Korea's February forecast of 2.2% and aligns with eight major investment banks' average

signals.headline: **Recency and Relevance**: This is the most recent forecast update in the results (April 6), post-March 2026 actual infl
signals.raw: **Recency and Relevance**: This is the most recent forecast update in the results (April 6), post-March 2026 actual inflation of 2.2% YoY (up from 2.0%, below 2.4% expected).[2][4][5] No confirmations, announcements, or resolutions after April 6 or today (April 14) appear in results.

signals.headline: **Prediction Market Impact**: Upward inflation revisions could shift markets on Bank of Korea rate cut probabilities (he
signals.raw: **Prediction Market Impact**: Upward inflation revisions could shift markets on Bank of Korea rate cut probabilities (held at 2.5% in recent meetings amid oil-driven pressures),[3][4] though medium confidence due to no post-April 6 data and AMRO's regional (not official BOK) status.[1][2]
